title: Governance Documents
text:
This page contains founding documents for the formation of a California Public Benefit corporation and Internal Revenue Service tax exemption under section 501(c)(3): the Generation of Participation in Democracy.
We are a California Public Benefit corporation in good standing. Our application for 501(c)(3) tax exemption has been submitted for review by the IRS.
To apply for the 501(c)(3) exemption there must be a board with a president, secretary, and treasurer. For now, Matthew A. Turner, PhD is the sole board member filling all three roles. This is temporary.
Dr. Turner will serve as President of the GPD for the foreseeable future. Dr. Turner is currently interviewing candidates to fill the roles of Secretary and Treasurer.
The documents below establish sole directorship, bylaws, and a conflict of interest policy.


UNANIMOUS WRITTEN CONSENT OF THE SOLE DIRECTOR OF GENERATION OF PARTICIPATION IN DEMOCRACY
The undersigned, being the sole director of Generation of Participation in Democracy, a California nonprofit public benefit corporation, hereby adopts the following resolutions by unanimous written consent pursuant to applicable California law.


RESOLVED, that the bylaws presented to the director are hereby adopted as the bylaws of the corporation (see the Bylaws section below).



RESOLVED, that the following officers are appointed to serve until resignation, removal, or successor appointment:
President: Matthew A. Turner, PhD Secretary: Matthew A. Turner, PhD Treasurer: Matthew A. Turner, PhD



RESOLVED, that the Conflict of Interest Policy presented to the director is hereby adopted (see below).



RESOLVED, that the officers of the corporation are authorized and directed to prepare and submit Form 1023 and any related filings necessary to obtain recognition of exemption under Section 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code.



RESOLVED, that the officers are authorized to establish bank accounts and conduct ordinary financial operations on behalf of the corporation.



RESOLVED, that all actions previously taken on behalf of the corporation consistent with these resolutions are ratified and approved.
DATED: 11 May 2026

Matthew A. Turner, PhD
President & Sole Director




BYLAWS OF GENERATION OF PARTICIPATION IN DEMOCRACY


The name of this corporation is Generation of Participation in Democracy.



This corporation is organized exclusively for charitable, educational, scientific, and related exempt purposes within the meaning of Section 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code.



The principal office of the corporation shall be located in California or such other location as determined by the Board of Directors.





The affairs of the corporation shall be managed by its Board of Directors.



The authorized number of directors shall be not less than one (1) nor more than seven (7), with the exact number fixed by resolution of the Board.



Directors shall serve until resignation, removal, or successor appointment.



Meetings may be held in person or by electronic communication.



Any action required or permitted to be taken by the Board may be taken without a meeting if written consent is provided.




The officers of the corporation shall include a President, Secretary, and Treasurer, and may include additional officers as determined by the Board.



The corporation shall maintain and comply with a Conflict of Interest Policy.



No part of the net earnings of the corporation shall inure to the benefit of any private individual except for reasonable compensation for services rendered.
The corporation shall not participate or intervene in any political campaign on behalf of or in opposition to any candidate for public office.



Upon dissolution, the assets of the corporation shall be distributed for one or more exempt purposes within the meaning of Section 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code.



These bylaws may be amended by the Board of Directors.



These bylaws were adopted on 11 May 2026.

Matthew Turner
Secretary




CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY OF THE GENERATION OF PARTICIPATION IN DEMOCRACY


The purpose of this policy is to protect the interests of the corporation when considering transactions or arrangements that may benefit the private interests of a director, officer, or key person.



Interested persons shall disclose any actual or potential conflicts of interest.



The Board shall determine whether a conflict exists and whether any proposed arrangement is fair, reasonable, and in the best interests of the corporation.



The minutes of the corporation shall document disclosures, determinations, and approvals related to conflicts of interest.



Compensation arrangements shall be reviewed and approved using reasonable and transparent procedures.



The corporation may collaborate with or receive operational support from affiliated entities, including Subtlety Solutions (STS). STS is an LLC owned by director, president, treasurer, and secretary Matthew Turner.
The corporation recognizes that overlapping leadership, infrastructure, technical services, or financial relationships may create actual or perceived conflicts of interest.
Such relationships are permitted only when fully disclosed, documented, and determined to be fair, reasonable, and in the best interests of the corporation.
The corporation therefore maintains a robust transparency and disclosure framework, including documented approvals, public-facing financial reporting where appropriate, and formal conflict review procedures intended to preserve the corporation’s charitable mission and public trust.
ADOPTED: 11 May 2026

Matthew Turner
Secretary

title: Support GPD Américas
text:
Your support enables the development of sustainable, participatory institutions across the Americas. Currently, 100% of contributions go to fund research, field programs, and infrastructure through our Flagship Institute, Instituto Dourados. Once we can fund the first two years of operation of Instituto Dourados we may consider expanding GPD’s institutional portfolio, allocating resources beyond just Instituto Dourados.
Opportunities to support other GPD Américas organizations will be announced as they become available.

title: Support GPD Américas
text:
Contribution Tiers
The following table lists the minimum contribution for six tiers of support. Different tiers have different perks, as indicated by superscripts and explained following the table.








Tier
Min. Contribution
Description




Founder\(^{1,2,3}\)
$1,000,000
Institutional founding support


Platinum\(^{1,2,3}\)
$100,000
Major program sponsorship


Gold\(^{1,2,3}\)
$10,000
Program and research support


Silver\(^{1,2}\)
$1,000
Travel and entry-level salary


Reader\(^{1}\)
$100
Help pay infrastructure costs


Hero
≥ $1
Help as you are able, and you’re our hero




Explanation of Tiers

Includes one-year subscription Instituto Dourados Quarterly Journal.
The donor’s name, or the name of someone the donor wants to honor, will be published on the website. The donor may add a short message as well.
These tiers are classified as a Major Contribution at GPD. Please contact our giving team to make personalized arrangements: giving@gpdamericas.org. We will be happy to welcome you as a Generation of Participation in Democracy partner if it is indeed a good fit for both parties.

All contributions are governed under our public benefit mission and transparency principles. Currently anticipated budget allocations and funding scenarios are published on the Instituto Dourados page.

title: Generation of Participation in Democracy (GPD)
text:
Generation of Participation in Democracy (GPD)


A Sustainable Operating System for the Americas


Public Benefit Mission. GPD is a California Public Benefit Corporation advancing sustainable, participatory societies across the Americas. Our specific public benefit is to strengthen democratic capacity, public health, and institutional resilience through integrated social science, technology, and field-based collaboration.


Transparency. Meaningful democratic participation requires transparency. GPD operates under a principle of radical transparency wherever possible—making methods, data practices, and institutional decisions legible and accountable to the communities we serve.


We scale efforts aligned with the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals, focusing on the foundations of participation: access to essential resources, reliable information, and secure institutions.


Indigenous Public Health — Instituto Dourados


Our first Flagship Institute, the Instituto Dourados, develops and deploys public health systems in collaboration with Indigenous communities, integrating epidemiology, social science, and multilingual knowledge sharing.


Education and Digital Security


We design educational systems and privacy-first technologies that enable institutions to analyze sensitive data locally, maintaining data sovereignty while supporting real-time decision-making.


Corporate Information


Legal Structure: California Public Benefit Corporation IRS Employer Identification Number (EIN): 41-4992916 Transparency inquiries/concerns: transparency@gpdamericas.org








Founder’s Statement


GPD is built to move from theory to implementation—combining research, infrastructure, and education to support sustainable futures across the Americas.



In solidarity and service,



Matt Turner, PhD


Founder & Architect, GPD Américas


Contact

title: Instituto Dourados
text: Instituto Dourados (institutodourados.org) is the Flagship Institute of the Generation of Participation in Democracy (GPD). As a Flagship, it serves as a primary site of development, testing, and deployment—where research, infrastructure, and field collaboration are integrated into working systems. The goal is not only to study sustainable participation, but to build and validate it in practice.

title: Instituto Dourados
text: Instituto Dourados (institutodourados.org) is the Flagship Institute of the Generation of Participation in Democracy (GPD). As a Flagship, it serves as a primary site of development, testing, and deployment—where research, infrastructure, and field collaboration are integrated into working systems. The goal is not only to study sustainable participation, but to build and validate it in practice.

title: Instituto Dourados
text:
Purpose and Plan (2026–2027)
This budget bridges Instituto Dourados from its current startup phase to autonomous operation with a 5-10 year operating endownment beginning January 2027. The 2026 bridge year prioritizes:

Director capacity (research, operations, and fundraising)
Field integration with Brazilian partners
Institutional visibility through conferences and collaboration


The objective for Instituto Dourados is to become a self-sustaining research exchange program by 2027. In 2027 we will expand staffing to support more student intern trainees and add a new field site, likely in the state of Bahia. Self-sustaining here means the Instituto’s endowment reaches a level to guarantee 5-10 years of a growth-oriented budget.

2026 Bridge Budget
This budget enables:

Three field visits to Brazil in 2026 (Q2–Q4)
Conference participation and partner coordination
On-site collaboration and exchange programming
Focused time allocation for institutional buildout

The 2026 bridge year is designed to:

Establish recurring funding streams
Expand partnerships and institutional visibility
Support initial hiring and program growth

title: Instituto Dourados
text:
Fiscal Structure
During the bridge period, Instituto Dourados may seek and operate under fiscal sponsorship while its parent organization, GPD, completes 501(c)(3) status.
An alternative pathway is direct administration through existing institutional accounts to support salary and travel during the transihhhtion period. We are opening our doors to the general public first, though, before seeking larger institutional investors.

title: Instituto Dourados
text:
Director Commitment
The directors commit to matching institutional support through active fundraising during 2026. This alignment ensures that bridge funding directly accelerates long-term sustainability and growth.

title: Instituto Dourados
text:
Outlook
Instituto Dourados is designed as a replicable model: a field-anchored institute that integrates research, infrastructure, and education to produce measurable improvements in public health and democratic participation.
